chunks.text: Berkeley Blue team advances to World Finals - EECS at Berkeley On Saturday, November 5, Berkeley hosted the 2016 Pacific Northwest Regional Programming Contest , part of the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest . In Division I , the Berkeley Blue team, comprising Keyhan Vakil, Evan Limanto, and Ruichao Chen, took second place, behind a team from the University of British Columbia (and ahead of the top Stanford team). In Division II , the Berkeley Ursi team, comprising Michael Luo, Larry Yang, and Eric Sheng, took first place. The Berkeley Blue team now advances to the World Finals to be held in Rapid City, South Dakota in May 2017. Categories Honors Students View Open Faculty Positions
